gene_description: Anti-Müllerian hormone (AMH) — TGF-beta superfamily cytokine produced by ovarian granulosa cells, surrogate marker for number of growing follicles and ovarian reserve

source.title: Meta-analysis of genome-wide association studies identifies common variants associated with circulating progesterone levels and explains their association with fertility
source.finding: rs10407022 T allele associated with higher serum AMH levels and more metaphase II oocytes retrieved during assisted reproduction. Effect modulated by ethnicity, with stronger effects in Asian populations.

TT.effect: Homozygous for T allele. Associated with higher circulating AMH levels, indicating larger pool of growing follicles and increased ovarian reserve. More metaphase II oocytes retrieved during fertility treatment. Correlates with later age at menopause (~1-2 years later).
TT.recommendation: Good ovarian reserve indicator. Favorable response anticipated to fertility medications if assisted reproduction needed. Standard reproductive planning applies. Monitor AMH levels periodically.

GT.effect: Heterozygous for T/G alleles. Intermediate AMH levels and ovarian reserve. Intermediate response to fertility stimulation. Normal fertility expected in most cases.
GT.recommendation: Standard reproductive assessment and planning. AMH testing recommended if evaluating fertility or approaching assisted reproduction.

TG.effect: Heterozygous for T/G alleles. Intermediate AMH levels and ovarian reserve. Intermediate response to fertility stimulation. Normal fertility expected in most cases.
TG.recommendation: Standard reproductive assessment and planning. AMH testing recommended if evaluating fertility or approaching assisted reproduction.

GG.effect: Homozygous for G allele (wild-type). Associated with lower AMH levels and reduced ovarian follicle pool. Fewer oocytes retrieved during assisted reproduction. Correlates with earlier age at menopause.
GG.recommendation: If concerned about fertility, AMH testing and ovarian reserve assessment recommended. Early oocyte or embryo preservation may be considered. Earlier reproductive planning may be prudent given smaller follicle pool.
